keyPoints: CPU Execution Time is the true measure of computer performance.
Instructions Per Cycle (IPC) is the reciprocal of Cycles Per Instruction (CPI).
Amdahl's Law dictates the maximum speedup possible from improving a part of a system.
commonMistakes: Confusing CPU Time with Throughput or Clock Rate alone.
Using the average CPI without weighting by instruction frequency.
Misapplying Amdahl's Law by focusing on the enhanced fraction instead of the unenhanced fraction.
